title: The Evidence Tribunal: Adversarial Specification Search Across 403 Cochrane Meta-Analyses
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can adversarial specification search quantify how much a meta-analytic conclusion depends on analyst choices rather than underlying evidence? The Evidence Tribunal applies prosecution-defence framing to 403 Cochrane reviews, where prosecution maximizes significance and defence minimizes it, across seven estimators, three confidence interval methods, and three bias corrections. For each review, the adversarial gap between strongest and weakest specifications is computed on effect-size and log-p-value scales, classified as narrow, moderate, wide, or extreme. Of 403 reviews, 266 (66 percent, 95% CI 61 to 71) received split verdicts where prosecution achieved significance but defence did not, while only 15 yielded unanimous significance. The mean adversarial gap was 0.651 with median 0.157, and PET-PEESE was the most common defence-favored bias correction. This framework reveals that most published meta-analytic conclusions are potentially vulnerable to reasonable alternative analytical specifications. However, the limitation of relying on pre-computed specification data means the current multiverse does not include Bayesian priors or alternative effect-size metrics.

sentences.text: Can adversarial specification search quantify how much a meta-analytic conclusion depends on analyst choices rather than underlying evidence?

The Evidence Tribunal applies prosecution-defence framing to 403 Cochrane reviews, where prosecution maximizes significance and defence minimizes it, across seven estimators, three confidence interval methods, and three bias corrections.
For each review, the adversarial gap between strongest and weakest specifications is computed on effect-size and log-p-value scales, classified as narrow, moderate, wide, or extreme.
Of 403 reviews, 266 (66 percent, 95% CI 61 to 71) received split verdicts where prosecution achieved significance but defence did not, while only 15 yielded unanimous significance.
The mean adversarial gap was 0.651 with median 0.157, and PET-PEESE was the most common defence-favored bias correction.
This framework reveals that most published meta-analytic conclusions are potentially vulnerable to reasonable alternative analytical specifications.
However, the limitation of relying on pre-computed specification data means the current multiverse does not include Bayesian priors or alternative effect-size metrics.

notes.data: 403 Cochrane reviews via Fragility Atlas specifications

checks.name: single paragraph
checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.name: no headings
checks.detail: No markdown headings allowed in body.

checks.name: no links
checks.detail: No links or DOI links allowed in body.

checks.name: result sentence has interval
checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.detail: Sentence 4 should name an effect measure or test metric.
